question: What's the best time to visit Clare Valley SA with kids?
answer: G'day! Spring (September to November) is absolutely brilliant for families - mild weather perfect for little ones, beautiful wildflowers, and not too crowded. Autumn's lovely too, though busier with wine tourists. Summer can get pretty toasty (30°C+), so pack the sunscreen and hit the water parks early. Winter's cool but cosy - great for cuddling up in your cabin after exploring!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Clare Valley SA?
answer: You bet! Several Clare Valley holiday parks welcome your furry family members. Most require pets to be kept on leads and have designated areas. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees - usually around $10-20 per night. Some cabins are pet-free zones, so booking early ensures you'll snag a pet-friendly spot for the whole gang!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Clare Valley SA during school holidays?
answer: School holiday rates typically range from $120-250 per night depending on accommodation type. Powered sites start around $50-70, while family cabins run $150-250+. Prices peak during Easter and summer Christmas holidays. Book at least 2-3 months ahead for better rates and availability. Shoulder season visits can save you 20-30% - bonus for families on a budget!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Don't forget the essentials, mate! Pack bedding (most cabins need BYO linen), toiletries, insect repellent, sunscreen, and hats. Bring outdoor toys, bikes, or scooters - parks have loads of space! Kitchen basics like tea towels and dishwashing liquid are handy. Kids' favourite snacks, first aid kit, and entertainment for rainy days. Oh, and spare clothes - kids and mud are best mates!

question: What is there to do near Clare Valley holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps of fun for the littlies! The Riesling Trail is perfect for family bike rides with playgrounds along the way. Visit local farms, try berry picking in season, or explore gorgeous spots like Spring Gully Conservation Park. Many wineries have family-friendly facilities and lawns for picnics. The towns have great bakeries, shops, and swimming pools - plenty to keep everyone happy!
